title: Canto 10 Chapter 41
summary: Complete English Bhagavatam chapter generated from the public gita/Datasets repository. Each route exposes one shloka at a time.
sourceNote: This chapter includes devanagari text, roman transliteration, English translation, and purport when available in the upstream corpus.

verses.text: श्रीशुक उवाचस्तुवतस्तस्य भगवान् दर्शयित्वा जले वपु: ।भूय: समाहरत् कृष्णो नटो नाट्यमिवात्मन: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācastuvatas tasya bhagavāndarśayitvā jale vapuḥbhūyaḥ samāharat kṛṣṇonaṭo nāṭyam ivātmanaḥ
verses.meaning: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: While Akrūra was still offering prayers, the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa withdrew His form that He had revealed in the water, just as an actor winds up his performance.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; śukaḥ; uvāca; stuvataḥ; tasya; bhagavān; darśayitvā; jale; vapuḥ; bhūyaḥ; samāharat; kṛṣṇaḥ; naṭaḥ; nāṭyam; iva; ātmanaḥ
verses.purport: Lord Kṛṣṇa withdrew from Akrūra’s sight the Viṣṇu form along with the vision of the spiritual sky and its eternal inhabitants.

verses.text: सोऽपि चान्तर्हितं वीक्ष्य जलादुन्मज्य सत्वर: ।कृत्वा चावश्यकं सर्वं विस्मितो रथमागमत् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’pi cāntarhitaṁ vīkṣyajalād unmajya satvaraḥkṛtvā cāvaśyakaṁ sarvaṁvismito ratham āgamat
verses.meaning: When Akrūra saw the vision disappear, he came out of the water and quickly finished his various ritual duties. He then returned to the chariot, astonished.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; api; ca; antarhitam; vīkṣya; jalāt; unmajya; satvaraḥ; kṛtvā; ca; āvaśyakam; sarvam; vismitaḥ; ratham; āgamat

verses.text: तमपृच्छद्‌धृषीकेश: किं ते द‍ृष्टमिवाद्भ‍ुतम् ।भूमौ वियति तोये वा तथा त्वां लक्षयामहे ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam apṛcchad dhṛṣīkeśaḥkiṁ te dṛṣṭam ivādbhutambhūmau viyati toye vātathā tvāṁ lakṣayāmahe
verses.meaning: Lord Kṛṣṇa asked Akrūra: Have you seen something wonderful on the earth, in the sky or in the water? From your appearance, We think you have.
verses.wordMeanings: tam; apṛcchat; hṛṣīkśaḥ; kim; te; dṛṣṭam; iva; adbhutam; bhūmau; viyati; toye; va; tathā; tvām; lakṣayāmahe

verses.text: श्रीअक्रूर उवाचअद्भ‍ुतानीह यावन्ति भूमौ वियति वा जले ।त्वयि विश्वात्मके तानि किं मेऽद‍ृष्टं विपश्यत: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-akrūra uvācaadbhutānīha yāvantibhūmau viyati vā jaletvayi viśvātmake tānikiṁ me ’dṛṣṭaṁ vipaśyataḥ
verses.meaning: Śrī Akrūra said: Whatever wonderful things the earth, sky or water contain, all exist in You. Since You encompass everything, when I am seeing You, what have I not seen?
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; akrūraḥ; uvāca; adbhutāni; iha; yāvanti; bhūmau; viyati; vā; jale; tvayi; viśva; ātmake; tāni; kim; me; adṛṣṭam; vipaśyataḥ

verses.text: यत्राद्भ‍ुतानि सर्वाणि भूमौ वियति वा जले ।तं त्वानुपश्यतो ब्रह्मन् किं मे द‍ृष्टमिहाद्भ‍ुतम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yatrādbhutāni sarvāṇibhūmau viyati vā jaletaṁ tvānupaśyato brahmankiṁ me dṛṣṭam ihādbhutam
verses.meaning: And now that I am seeing You, O Supreme Absolute Truth, in whom reside all amazing things on the earth, in the sky and in the water, what amazing things could I see in this world?
verses.wordMeanings: yatra; adbhutāni; sarvāṇi; bhūmau; viyati; vā; jale; tam; tvā; anupaśyataḥ; brahman; kim; me; dṛṣṭam; iha; adbhutam
verses.purport: Akrūra has now realized that Lord Kṛṣṇa is not merely his nephew.

verses.text: इत्युक्त्वा चोदयामास स्यन्दनं गान्दिनीसुत: ।मथुरामनयद् रामं कृष्णं चैव दिनात्यये ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktvā codayām āsasyandanaṁ gāndinī-sutaḥmathurām anayad rāmaṁkṛṣṇaṁ caiva dinātyaye
verses.meaning: With these words, Akrūra, the son of Gāndinī, began driving the chariot onward. At the end of the day he arrived in Mathurā with Lord Balarāma and Lord Kṛṣṇa.
verses.wordMeanings: iti; uktvā; codayām; āsa; syandanam; gāndinī; sutaḥ; mathurām; anayat; rāmam; kṛṣṇam; ca; eva; dina; atyaye

verses.text: मार्गे ग्रामजना राजंस्तत्र तत्रोपसङ्गता: ।वसुदेवसुतौ वीक्ष्य प्रीता द‍ृष्टिं न चाददु: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: mārge grāma-janā rājaṁstatra tatropasaṅgatāḥvasudeva-sutau vīkṣyaprītā dṛṣṭiṁ na cādaduḥ
verses.meaning: Wherever they passed along the road, O King, the village people came forward and looked upon the two sons of Vasudeva with great pleasure. In fact, the villagers could not withdraw their eyes from Them.
verses.wordMeanings: mārge; grāma; janāḥ; rājan; tatra; tatra; upasaṅgatāḥ; vasudeva; sutau; vīkṣya; prītāḥ; dṛṣṭim; na; ca; ādaduḥ

verses.text: तावद् व्रजौकसस्तत्र नन्दगोपादयोऽग्रत: ।पुरोपवनमासाद्य प्रतीक्षन्तोऽवतस्थिरे ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāvad vrajaukasas tatrananda-gopādayo ’grataḥpuropavanam āsādyapratīkṣanto ’vatasthire
verses.meaning: Nanda Mahārāja and the other residents of Vṛndāvana, having reached Mathurā ahead of the chariot, had stopped at a garden on the outskirts of the city to wait for Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma.
verses.wordMeanings: tāvat; vraja; okasaḥ; tatra; nanda; gopa; ādayaḥ; agrataḥ; pura; upavanam; āsādya; pratīkṣantaḥ; avatasthire
verses.purport: Nanda and the others reached Mathurā first because the chariot carrying Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma was delayed by Akrūra’s bathing.

verses.text: तान् समेत्याह भगवानक्रूरं जगदीश्वर: ।गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणिं प्रश्रितं प्रहसन्निव ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tān sametyāha bhagavānakrūraṁ jagad-īśvaraḥgṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇiṁpraśritaṁ prahasann iva
verses.meaning: After joining Nanda and the others, the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa, the controller of the universe, took humble Akrūra’s hand in His own and, smiling, spoke as follows.
verses.wordMeanings: tān; sametya; āha; bhagavān; akrūram; jagat; īśvaraḥ; gṛhītvā; pāṇinā; pāṇim; praśritam; prahasan; iva

verses.text: भवान् प्रविशतामग्रे सहयान: पुरीं गृहम् ।वयं त्विहावमुच्याथ ततो द्रक्ष्यामहे पुरीम् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: bhavān praviśatām agresaha-yānaḥ purīṁ gṛhamvayaṁ tv ihāvamucyāthatato drakṣyāmahe purīm
verses.meaning: [Lord Kṛṣṇa said:] Take the chariot and enter the city ahead of us. Then go home. After resting here a while, we will go to see the city.
verses.wordMeanings: bhavān; praviśatām; agre; saha; yānaḥ; purīm; gṛham; vayam; tu; iha; avamucya; atha; tataḥ; drakṣyāmahe; purīm

verses.text: श्रीअक्रूर उवाचनाहं भवद्‌भ्यां रहित: प्रवेक्ष्ये मथुरां प्रभो ।त्यक्तुं नार्हसि मां नाथ भक्तं ते भक्तवत्सल ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-akrūra uvācanāhaṁ bhavadbhyāṁ rahitaḥpravekṣye mathurāṁ prabhotyaktuṁ nārhasi māṁ nāthabhaktaṁ te bhakta-vatsala
verses.meaning: Śrī Akrūra said: O master, without the two of You I shall not enter Mathurā. I am Your devotee, O Lord, so it is not fair for You to abandon me, since You are always affectionate to Your devotees.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; akrūraḥ; uvāca; na; aham; bhavadbhyām; rahitaḥ; pravekṣye; mathurām; prabho; tyaktum; na; arhasi; mām; nātha; bhaktam; te; bhakta; vatsala

verses.text: आगच्छ याम गेहान्न: सनाथान्कुर्वधोक्षज ।सहाग्रज: सगोपालै: सुहृद्भ‍िश्च सुहृत्तम ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: āgaccha yāma gehān naḥsa-nāthān kurv adhokṣajasahāgrajaḥ sa-gopālaiḥsuhṛdbhiś ca suhṛttama
verses.meaning: Come, let us go to my house with Your elder brother, the cowherd men and Your companions. O best of friends, O transcendental Lord, in this way please grace my house with its master.
verses.wordMeanings: āgaccha; yāma; gehān; naḥ; sa; nāthān; kuru; adhokṣaja; saha; agra; jaḥ; sa; gopālaiḥ; suhṛdbhiḥ; ca; suhṛt; tama

verses.text: पुनीहि पादरजसा गृहान् नो गृहमेधिनाम् ।यच्छौचेनानुतृप्यन्ति पितर: साग्नय: सुरा: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: punīhi pāda-rajasāgṛhān no gṛha-medhināmyac-chaucenānutṛpyantipitaraḥ sāgnayaḥ surāḥ
verses.meaning: I am simply an ordinary householder attached to ritual sacrifices, so please purify my home with the dust of Your lotus feet. By that act of purification, my forefathers, the sacrificial fires and the demigods will all become satisfied.
verses.wordMeanings: punīhi; pāda; rajasā; gṛhān; naḥ; gṛha; medhinām; yat; śaucena; anutṛpyanti; pitaraḥ; sa; agnayaḥ; surāḥ

verses.text: अवनिज्याङ्‍‍‍‍‍घ्रियुगलमासीत्श्लोक्यो बलिर्महान् ।ऐश्वर्यमतुलं लेभे गतिं चैकान्तिनां तु या ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: avanijyāṅghri-yugalamāsīt ślokyo balir mahānaiśvaryam atulaṁ lebhegatiṁ caikāntināṁ tu yā
verses.meaning: By bathing Your feet, the exalted Bali Mahārāja attained not only glorious fame and unequaled power but also the final destination of pure devotees.
verses.wordMeanings: avanijya; aṅghri; yugalam; āsīt; ślokyaḥ; baliḥ; mahān; aiśvaryam; atulam; lebhe; gatīm; ca; ekāntinām; tu; yā

verses.text: आपस्तेऽङ्‌घ्य्रवनेजन्यस्त्रींल्लोकान् शुचयोऽपुनन् ।शिरसाधत्त या: शर्व: स्वर्याता: सगरात्मजा: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: āpas te ’ṅghry-avanejanyastrīḻ lokān śucayo ’punanśirasādhatta yāḥ śarvaḥsvar yātāḥ sagarātmajāḥ
verses.meaning: The water of the river Ganges has purified the three worlds, having become transcendental by bathing Your feet. Lord Śiva accepted that water on his head, and by that water’s grace the sons of King Sagara attained to heaven.
verses.wordMeanings: āpaḥ; te; aṅghri; avanejanyaḥ; trīn; lokān; śucayaḥ; apunan; śirasā; ādhatta; yāḥ; śarvaḥ; svaḥ; yātāḥ; sagara; ātmajāḥ

verses.text: देवदेव जगन्नाथ पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तन ।यदूत्तमोत्तम:श्लोक नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: deva-deva jagan-nāthapuṇya-śravaṇa-kīrtanayadūttamottamaḥ-ślokanārāyaṇa namo ’stu te
verses.meaning: O Lord of lords, master of the universe, O You whose glories it is most pious to hear and chant! O best of the Yadus, O You whose fame is recounted in excellent poetry! O Supreme Lord Nārāyaṇa, I offer You my obeisances.
verses.wordMeanings: deva; deva; jagat; nātha; puṇya; śravaṇa; kīrtana; yadu; uttama; uttamaḥ; śloka; nārāyaṇa; namaḥ; astu; te

verses.text: श्रीभगवानुवाचआयास्ये भवतो गेहमहमार्यसमन्वित: ।यदुचक्रद्रुहं हत्वा वितरिष्ये सुहृत्प्रियम् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācaāyāsye bhavato gehamaham arya-samanvitaḥyadu-cakra-druhaṁ hatvāvitariṣye suhṛt-priyam
verses.meaning: The Supreme Lord said: I will come to Your house with My elder brother, but first I must satisfy My friends and well-wishers by killing the enemy of the Yadu clan.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; bhagavān; uvāca; āyāsye; bhavataḥ; geham; aham; ārya; samanvitaḥ; yadu; cakra; druham; hatvā; vitariṣye; suhṛt; priyam
verses.purport: Akrūra glorified Kṛṣṇa in text 16 as yadūttama, “the best of the Yadus.” Śrī Kṛṣṇa here confirms this by saying, in effect, “Since I am the best of the Yadus, I must kill the enemy of the Yadus, Kaṁsa, and then I will come to your house.”

verses.text: श्रीशुक उवाचएवमुक्तो भगवता सोऽक्रूरो विमना इव ।पुरीं प्रविष्ट: कंसाय कर्मावेद्य गृहं ययौ ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevam ukto bhagavatāso ’krūro vimanā ivapurīṁ praviṣṭaḥ kaṁsāyakarmāvedya gṛhaṁ yayau
verses.meaning: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Thus addressed by the Lord, Akrūra entered the city with a heavy heart. He informed King Kaṁsa of the success of his mission and then went home.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; śukaḥ; uvāca; evam; uktaḥ; bhagavatā; saḥ; akrūraḥ; vimanāḥ; iva; purīm; praviṣṭaḥ; kaṁsāya; karma; āvedya; gṛham; yayau

verses.text: अथापराह्ने भगवान् कृष्ण: सङ्कर्षणान्वित: ।मथुरां प्राविशद् गोपैर्दिद‍ृक्षु: परिवारित: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: athāparāhne bhagavānkṛṣṇaḥ saṅkarṣaṇānvitaḥmathurāṁ prāviśad gopairdidṛkṣuḥ parivāritaḥ
verses.meaning: Lord Kṛṣṇa desired to see Mathurā, so toward evening He took Lord Balarāma and the cowherd boys with Him and entered the city.
verses.wordMeanings: atha; apara; ahne; bhagavān; kṛṣṇaḥ; saṅkarṣaṇa; anvitaḥ; mathurām; prāviśat; gopaiḥ; didṛkṣuḥ; parivāritaḥ

verses.text: ददर्श तां स्फाटिकतुङ्गगोपुर-द्वारां बृहद्धेमकपाटतोरणाम् ।ताम्रारकोष्ठां परिखादुरासदा-मुद्यानरम्योपवनोपशोभिताम् ॥ २० ॥सौवर्णश‍ृङ्गाटकहर्म्यनिष्कुटै:श्रेणीसभाभिर्भवनैरुपस्कृताम् ।वैदूर्यवज्रामलनीलविद्रुमै-र्मुक्ताहरिद्भ‍िर्वलभीषु वेदिषु ॥ २१ ॥जुष्टेषु जालामुखरन्ध्रकुट्टिमे-ष्वाविष्टपारावतबर्हिनादिताम् ।संसिक्तरथ्यापणमार्गचत्वरांप्रकीर्णमाल्याङ्कुरलाजतण्डुलाम् ॥ २२ ॥आपूर्णकुम्भैर्दधिचन्दनोक्षितै:प्रसूनदीपावलिभि: सपल्लवै: ।सवृन्दरम्भाक्रमुकै: सकेतुभि:स्वलङ्कृतद्वारगृहां सपट्टिकै: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: dadarśa tāṁ sphāṭika-tuṇga-gopura-dvārāṁ bṛhad-dhema-kapāṭa-toraṇāmtāmrāra-koṣṭhāṁ parikhā-durāsadāmudyāna-ramyopavanopaśobhitām
verses.meaning: The Lord saw Mathurā, with its tall gates and household entrances made of crystal, its immense archways and main doors of gold, its granaries and other storehouses of copper and brass, and its impregnable moats. Beautifying the city were pleasant gardens and parks. The main intersections were fashioned of gold, and there were mansions with private pleasure gardens, along with guildhalls and many other buildings. Mathurā resounded with the calls of peacocks and pet turtledoves, who sat in the small openings of the lattice windows and on the gem-studded floors, and also on the columned balconies and on the ornate rafters in front of the houses. These balconies and rafters were adorned with vaidūrya stones, diamonds, crystal quartz, sapphires, coral, pearls and emeralds. All the royal avenues and commercial streets were sprinkled with water, as were the side roads and courtyards, and flower garlands, newly grown sprouts, parched grains and rice had been scattered about everywhere. Gracing the houses’ doorways were elaborately decorated pots filled with water, which were bedecked with mango leaves, smeared with yogurt and sandalwood paste, and encircled by flower petals and ribbons. Near the pots were flags, rows of lamps, bunches of flowers and the trunks of banana and betel-nut trees.
verses.wordMeanings: dadarśa; tam; sphāṭika; tuṅga; gopura; dvārām; bṛhat; hema; kapāṭa; toraṇām; tāmra; āra; koṣṭhām; parikhā; durāsadām; udyāna; ramya; upavana; upaśobhitam; sauvarṇa; śṛṅgāṭaka; harmya; niṣkuṭaiḥ; śreṇī; sabhābhiḥ; bhavanaiḥ; upaskṛtām; vaidūrya; vajra; amala; nīla; vidrumaiḥ; muktā; haridbhiḥ; valabhīṣu; vediṣu; juṣṭeṣu; jāla; āmukha; randhra; kuṭṭimeṣu; āviṣṭa; pārāvata; barhi; nāditām; saṁsikta; rathyā; āpaṇa; mārga; catvarām; prakīrṇa; mālya; aṅkura; lāja; taṇḍulām; āpūrṇa; kumbhaiḥ; dadhi; candana; ukṣitaiḥ; prasūna; dīpa; āvalibhiḥ; sa; pallavaiḥ; sa; vṛnda; rambhā; kramukaiḥ; sa; ketubhiḥ; su; alaṅkṛta; dvāra; gṛhām; sa; paṭṭikaiḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura gives this description of the elaborately decorated pots: “On either side of each doorway, above the scattered rice, is a pot. Encircling each pot are flower petals, on its neck are ribbons and in its mouth are leaves of mango and other trees. Above each pot, on a gold plate, are rows of lamps. A trunk of a banana tree stands on either side of each pot, and a betel-nut tree trunk stands in front and also behind. Flags lean against the pots.”

verses.text: तां सम्प्रविष्टौ वसुदेवनन्दनौवृतौ वयस्यैर्नरदेववर्त्मना ।द्रष्टुं समीयुस्त्वरिता: पुरस्त्रियोहर्म्याणि चैवारुरुहुर्नृपोत्सुका: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāṁ sampraviṣṭau vasudeva-nandanauvṛtau vayasyair naradeva-vartmanādraṣṭuṁ samīyus tvaritāḥ pura-striyoharmyāṇi caivāruruhur nṛpotsukāḥ
verses.meaning: The women of Mathurā hurriedly assembled and went forth to see the two sons of Vasudeva as They entered the city on the King’s road, surrounded by Their cowherd boyfriends. Some of the women, my dear King, eagerly climbed to the roofs of their houses to see Them.
verses.wordMeanings: tām; sampraviṣṭau; vasudeva; nandanau; vṛtau; vayasyaiḥ; nara; deva; vartmanā; draṣṭum; samīyuḥ; tvaritāḥ; pura; striyaḥ; harmyāṇi; ca; eva; āruruhuḥ; nṛpa; utsukāḥ

verses.text: काश्‍चिद् विपर्यग्धृतवस्त्रभूषणाविस्मृत्य चैकं युगलेष्वथापरा: ।कृतैकपत्रश्रवनैकनूपुरानाङ्‌क्त्वा द्वितीयं त्वपराश्च लोचनम् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kāścid viparyag-dhṛta-vastra-bhūṣaṇāvismṛtya caikaṁ yugaleṣv athāparāḥkṛtaika-patra-śravanaika-nūpurānāṅktvā dvitīyaṁ tv aparāś ca locanam
verses.meaning: Some of the ladies put their clothes and ornaments on backwards, others forgot one of their earrings or ankle bells, and others applied makeup to one eye but not the other.
verses.wordMeanings: kaścit; viparyak; dhṛta; vastra; bhūṣaṇaḥ; vismṛtya; ca; ekam; yugaleṣu; atha; aparāḥ; kṛta; eka; patra; śravaṇa; eka; nūpurāḥ; na; aṅktvā; dvitīyam; tu; aparāḥ; ca; locanam
verses.purport: The ladies were very eager to see Kṛṣṇa, and in their haste and excitement they forgot themselves.

verses.text: अश्न‍न्त्य एकास्तदपास्य सोत्सवाअभ्यज्यमाना अकृतोपमज्जना: ।स्वपन्त्य उत्थाय निशम्य नि:स्वनंप्रपाययन्त्योऽर्भमपोह्य मातर: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: aśnantya ekās tad apāsya sotsavāabhyajyamānā akṛtopamajjanāḥsvapantya utthāya niśamya niḥsvanaṁprapāyayantyo ’rbham apohya mātaraḥ
verses.meaning: Those who were taking their meals abandoned them, others went out without finishing their baths or massages, women who were sleeping at once rose when they heard the commotion, and mothers breast-feeding their infants simply put them aside.
verses.wordMeanings: aśnantyaḥ; ekaḥ; tat; apāsya; sa; utsavaḥ; abhyajyamānāḥ; akṛta; upamajjanāḥ; svapantyaḥ; utthāya; niśamya; niḥsvanam; prapāyayantyaḥ; arbham; apohya; mātaraḥ

verses.text: मनांसि तासामरविन्दलोचन:प्रगल्भलीलाहसितावलोकै: ।जहार मत्तद्विरदेन्द्रविक्रमोद‍ृशां ददच्छ्रीरमणात्मनोत्सवम् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: manāṁsi tāsām aravinda-locanaḥpragalbha-līlā-hasitāvalokaiḥjahāra matta-dviradendra-vikramodṛśāṁ dadac chrī-ramaṇātmanotsavam
verses.meaning: The lotus-eyed Lord, smiling as He recalled His bold pastimes, captivated those ladies’ minds with His glances. He walked with the gait of a lordly elephant in rut, creating a festival for their eyes with His transcendental body, which is the source of pleasure for the divine goddess of fortune.
verses.wordMeanings: manāṁsi; tāsām; aravinda; locanaḥ; pragalbha; līlā; hasita; avalokaiḥ; jahāra; matta; dvirada; indra; vikramaḥ; dṛśām; dadat; śrī; ramaṇa; ātmanā; utsavam

verses.text: द‍ृष्ट्वा मुहु: श्रुतमनुद्रुतचेतसस्तंतत्प्रेक्षणोत्स्मितसुधोक्षणलब्धमाना: ।आनन्दमूर्तिमुपगुह्य द‍ृशात्मलब्धंहृष्यत्त्वचो जहुरनन्तमरिन्दमाधिम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā muhuḥ śrutam anudruta-cetasastaṁ tat-prekṣaṇotsmita-sudhokṣaṇa-labdha-mānāḥānanda-mūrtim upaguhya dṛśātma-labdhaṁhṛṣyat-tvaco jahur anantam arindamādhim
verses.meaning: The ladies of Mathurā had repeatedly heard about Kṛṣṇa, and thus as soon as they saw Him their hearts melted. They felt honored that He was sprinkling upon them the nectar of His glances and broad smiles. Taking Him into their hearts through their eyes, they embraced Him, the embodiment of all ecstasy, and as their bodily hairs stood on end, O subduer of enemies, they forgot the unlimited distress caused by His absence.
verses.wordMeanings: dṛṣṭvā; muhuḥ; śrutam; anudruta; cetasaḥ; tam; tat; prekṣaṇa; ut; smita; sudhā; ukṣaṇa; labdha; mānāḥ; ānanda; mūrtim; upaguhya; dṛśā; ātma; labdham; hṛṣyat; tvacaḥ; jahuḥ; anantam; arim; dama; ādhim

verses.text: प्रासादशिखरारूढा: प्रीत्युत्फुल्लमुखाम्बुजा: ।अभ्यवर्षन् सौमनस्यै: प्रमदा बलकेशवौ ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāsāda-śikharārūḍhāḥprīty-utphulla-mukhāmbujāḥabhyavarṣan saumanasyaiḥpramadā bala-keśavau
verses.meaning: Their lotus faces blooming with affection, the ladies who had climbed to the roofs of the mansions rained down showers of flowers upon Lord Balarāma and Lord Kṛṣṇa.
verses.wordMeanings: prāsāda; śikhara; ārūḍhāḥ; prīti; utphulla; mukha; ambujāḥ; abhyavarṣan; saumanasyaiḥ; pramadāḥ; bala; keśavau

verses.text: दध्यक्षतै: सोदपात्रै: स्रग्गन्धैरभ्युपायनै: ।तावानर्चु: प्रमुदितास्तत्र तत्र द्विजातय: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: dadhy-akṣataiḥ soda-pātraiḥsrag-gandhair abhyupāyanaiḥtāv ānarcuḥ pramuditāstatra tatra dvijātayaḥ
verses.meaning: Brāhmaṇas standing along the way honored the two Lords with presentations of yogurt, unbroken barleycorns, pots full of water, garlands, fragrant substances such as sandalwood paste, and other items of worship.
verses.wordMeanings: dadhi; akṣataiḥ; sa; uda; pātraiḥ; srak; gandhaiḥ; abhyupāyanaiḥ; tau; ānarcuḥ; pramuditāḥ; tatra; tatra; dvi; jātayaḥ

verses.text: ऊचु: पौरा अहो गोप्यस्तप: किमचरन्महत् ।या ह्येतावनुपश्यन्ति नरलोकमहोत्सवौ ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ūcuḥ paurā aho gopyastapaḥ kim acaran mahatyā hy etāv anupaśyantinara-loka-mahotsavau
verses.meaning: The women of Mathurā exclaimed: Oh, what severe austerities the gopīs must have performed to be able to regularly see Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, who are the greatest source of pleasure for all mankind!
verses.wordMeanings: ūcuḥ; paurāḥ; aho; gopyaḥ; tapaḥ; kim; acaran; mahat; yāḥ; hi; etau; anupaśyanti; nara; loka; mahā; utsavau

verses.text: रजकं कञ्चिदायान्तं रङ्गकारं गदाग्रज: ।द‍ृष्ट्वायाचत वासांसि धौतान्यत्युत्तमानि च ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: rajakaṁ kañcid āyāntaṁraṅga-kāraṁ gadāgrajaḥdṛṣṭvāyācata vāsāṁsidhautāny aty-uttamāni ca
verses.meaning: Seeing a washerman approaching who had been dyeing some clothes, Kṛṣṇa asked him for the finest laundered garments he had.
verses.wordMeanings: rajakam; kañcit; āyāntam; raṅga; kāram; gada; agrajaḥ; dṛṣṭvā; ayācata; vāsāṁsi; dhautāni; ati; uttamāni; ca

verses.text: देह्यावयो: समुचितान्यङ्ग वासांसि चार्हतो: ।भविष्यति परं श्रेयो दातुस्ते नात्र संशय: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: dehy āvayoḥ samucitānyaṅga vāsāṁsi cārhatoḥbhaviṣyati paraṁ śreyodātus te nātra saṁśayaḥ
verses.meaning: [Lord Kṛṣṇa said:] Please give suitable garments to the two of Us, who certainly deserve them. If you grant this charity, you will undoubtedly receive the greatest benefit.
verses.wordMeanings: dehi; āvayoḥ; samucitāni; aṅga; vāsāṁsi; ca; arhatoḥ; bhaviṣyati; param; śreyaḥ; dātuḥ; te; na; atra; saṁśayaḥ

verses.text: स याचितो भगवता परिपूर्णेन सर्वत: ।साक्षेपं रुषित: प्राह भृत्यो राज्ञ: सुदुर्मद: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa yācito bhagavatāparipūrṇena sarvataḥsākṣepaṁ ruṣitaḥ prāhabhṛtyo rājñaḥ su-durmadaḥ
verses.meaning: Thus requested by the Supreme Lord, who is perfectly complete in all respects, that arrogant servant of the King became angry and replied insultingly.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; yācitaḥ; bhagavatā; paripūrṇena; sarvataḥ; sa; ākṣepam; ruṣitaḥ; prāha; bhṛtyaḥ; rājñaḥ; su; durmadaḥ

verses.text: ईद‍ृशान्येव वासांसि नित्यं गिरिवनेचर: ।परिधत्त किमुद्‌वृत्ता राजद्रव्याण्यभीप्सथ ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: īdṛśāny eva vāsāṁsīnityaṁ giri-vane-caraḥparidhatta kim udvṛttārāja-dravyāṇy abhīpsatha
verses.meaning: [The washerman said:] You impudent boys! You’re accustomed to roaming the mountains and forests, and yet You would dare put on such clothes as these! These are the King’s possessions You’re asking for!
verses.wordMeanings: īdṛśāni; eva; vāsāṁsi; nityam; giri; vane; carāḥ; paridhatta; kim; udvṛttāḥ; rāja; dravyāṇi; abhīpsatha

verses.text: याताशु बालिशा मैवं प्रार्थ्यं यदि जिजीवीषा ।बध्नन्ति घ्नन्ति लुम्पन्ति द‍ृप्तं राजकुलानि वै ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yātāśu bāliśā maivaṁprārthyaṁ yadi jijīvīṣābadhnanti ghnanti lumpantidṛptaṁ rāja-kulāni vai
verses.meaning: Fools, get out of here quickly! Don’t beg like this if You want to stay alive. When someone is too bold, the King’s men arrest him and kill him and take all his property.
verses.wordMeanings: yāta; āśu; bāliśaḥ; mā; evam; prārthyam; yadi; jijīviṣā; badhnanti; ghnanti; lumpanti; dṛptam; rāja; kulāni; vai

verses.text: एवं विकत्थमानस्य कुपितो देवकीसुत: ।रजकस्य कराग्रेण शिर: कायादपातयत् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ vikatthamānasyakupito devakī-sutaḥrajakasya karāgreṇaśiraḥ kāyād apātayat
verses.meaning: As the washerman thus spoke brazenly, the son of Devakī became angry, and then merely with His fingertips He separated the man’s head from his body.
verses.wordMeanings: evam; vikatthamānasya; kupitaḥ; devakī; sutaḥ; rajakasya; kara; agreṇa; śiraḥ; kāyāt; apātayat

verses.text: तस्यानुजीविन: सर्वे वास:कोशान्विसृज्य वै ।दुद्रुवु: सर्वतो मार्गं वासांसि जगृहेऽच्युत: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyānujīvinaḥ sarvevāsaḥ-kośān visṛjya vaidudruvuḥ sarvato mārgaṁvāsāṁsi jagṛhe ’cyutaḥ
verses.meaning: The washerman’s assistants all dropped their bundles of clothes and fled down the road, scattering in all directions. Lord Kṛṣṇa then took the clothes.
verses.wordMeanings: tasya; anujīvinaḥ; sarve; vāsaḥ; kośān; visṛjya; vai; dadruvuḥ; sarvataḥ; mārgam; vāsāṁsi; jagṛhe; acyutaḥ

verses.text: वसित्वात्मप्रिये वस्‍‍‍‍‍त्रे कृष्ण: सङ्कर्षणस्तथा ।शेषाण्यादत्त गोपेभ्यो विसृज्य भुवि कानिचित् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vasitvātma-priye vastrekṛṣṇaḥ saṅkarṣaṇas tathāśeṣāṇy ādatta gopebhyovisṛjya bhuvi kānicit
verses.meaning: Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma put on pairs of garments that especially pleased Them, and then Kṛṣṇa distributed the remaining clothes among the cowherd boys, leaving some scattered on the ground.
verses.wordMeanings: vasitvā; ātma; priye; vastre; kṛṣṇaḥ; saṅkarṣaṇaḥ; tathā; śeṣāṇi; ādatta; gopebhyaḥ; visṛjya; bhuvi; kānicit

verses.text: ततस्तु वायक: प्रीतस्तयोर्वेषमकल्पयत् ।विचित्रवर्णैश्चैलेयैराकल्पैरनुरूपत: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: tatas tu vāyakaḥ prītastayor veṣam akalpayatvicitra-varṇaiś caileyairākalpair anurūpataḥ
verses.meaning: Thereupon a weaver came forward and, feeling affection for the Lords, nicely adorned Their attire with cloth ornaments of various colors.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; tu; vāyakaḥ; prītaḥ; tayoḥ; veṣam; akalpayat; vicitra; varṇaiḥ; caileyaiḥ; ākalpaiḥ; anurūpataḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī explains that the weaver adorned the Lords with cloth armlets and earrings that looked just like jewels. The word anurūpataḥ indicates that the colors matched nicely.

verses.text: नानालक्षणवेषाभ्यां कृष्णरामौ विरेजतु: ।स्वलङ्कृतौ बालगजौ पर्वणीव सितेतरौ ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: nānā-lakṣaṇa-veṣābhyāṁkṛṣṇa-rāmau virejatuḥsv-alaṅkṛtau bāla-gajauparvaṇīva sitetarau
verses.meaning: Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma looked resplendent, each in His own unique, wonderfully ornamented outfit. They resembled a pair of young elephants, one white and the other black, decorated for a festive occasion.
verses.wordMeanings: nānā; lakṣaṇa; veṣābhyām; kṛṣṇa; rāmau; virejatuḥ; su; alaṅkṛtau; bāla; gajau; parvaṇi; iva; sita; itarau

verses.text: तस्य प्रसन्नो भगवान् प्रादात्सारूप्यमात्मन: ।श्रियं च परमां लोके बलैश्वर्यस्मृतीन्द्रियम् ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya prasanno bhagavānprādāt sārūpyam ātmanaḥśriyaṁ ca paramāṁ lokebalaiśvarya-smṛtīndriyam
verses.meaning: Pleased with the weaver, the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa blessed him that after death he would achieve the liberation of attaining a form like the Lord’s, and that while in this world he would enjoy supreme opulence, physical strength, influence, memory and sensory vigor.
verses.wordMeanings: tasya; prasannaḥ; bhagavān; prādāt; sārūpyam; ātmanaḥ; śriyam; ca; paramām; loke; bala; aiśvarya; smṛti; indriyam

verses.text: तत: सुदाम्नो भवनं मालाकारस्य जग्मतु: ।तौ द‍ृष्ट्वा स समुत्थाय ननाम शिरसा भुवि ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ sudāmno bhavanaṁmālā-kārasya jagmatuḥtau dṛṣṭvā sa samutthāyananāma śirasā bhuvi
verses.meaning: The two Lords then went to the house of the garland-maker Sudāmā. When Sudāmā saw Them he at once stood up and then bowed down, placing his head on the ground.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; sudāmnaḥ; bhavanam; mālā; kārasya; jagmatuḥ; tau; dṛṣṭvā; saḥ; samutthāya; nanāma; śirasā; bhuvi

verses.text: तयोरासनमानीय पाद्यं चार्घ्यार्हणादिभि: ।पूजां सानुगयोश्चक्रे स्रक्ताम्बूलानुलेपनै: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tayor āsanam ānīyapādyaṁ cārghyārhaṇādibhiḥpūjāṁ sānugayoś cakresrak-tāmbūlānulepanaiḥ
verses.meaning: After offering Them seats and bathing Their feet, Sudāmā worshiped Them and Their companions with arghya, garlands, pān, sandalwood paste and other presentations.
verses.wordMeanings: tayoḥ; āsanam; ānīya; pādyam; ca; arghya; arhaṇa; ādibhiḥ; pūjām; sa; anugayoḥ; cakre; srak; tāmbūla; anulepanaiḥ

verses.text: प्राह न: सार्थकं जन्म पावितं च कुलं प्रभो ।पितृदेवर्षयो मह्यं तुष्टा ह्यागमनेन वाम् ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāha naḥ sārthakaṁ janmapāvitaṁ ca kulaṁ prabhopitṛ-devarṣayo mahyaṁtuṣṭā hy āgamanena vām
verses.meaning: [Sudāmā said:] O Lord, my birth is now sanctified and my family free of contamination. Now that You both have come here, my forefathers, the demigods and the great sages are certainly all satisfied with me.
verses.wordMeanings: prāha; naḥ; sa; arthakam; janma; pāvitam; ca; kulam; prabho; pitṛ; deva; ṛṣayaḥ; mahyam; tuṣṭāḥ; hi; āgamanena; vām

verses.text: भवन्तौ किल विश्वस्य जगत: कारणं परम् ।अवतीर्णाविहांशेन क्षेमाय च भवाय च ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhavantau kila viśvasyajagataḥ kāraṇaṁ paramavatīrṇāv ihāṁśenakṣemāya ca bhavāya ca
verses.meaning: You two Lords are the ultimate cause of this entire universe. To bestow sustenance and prosperity upon this realm, You have descended with Your plenary expansions.
verses.wordMeanings: bhavantau; kila; viśvasya; jagataḥ; kāraṇam; param; avatīrṇau; iha; aṁśena; kṣemāya; ca; bhavāya; ca

verses.text: न हि वां विषमा द‍ृष्टि: सुहृदोर्जगदात्मनो: ।समयो: सर्वभूतेषु भजन्तं भजतोरपि ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hi vāṁ viṣamā dṛṣṭiḥsuhṛdor jagad-ātmanoḥsamayoḥ sarva-bhūteṣubhajantaṁ bhajator api
verses.meaning: Because You are the well-wishing friends and Supreme Soul of the whole universe, You regard all with unbiased vision. Therefore, although You reciprocate Your devotees’ loving worship, You always remain equally disposed toward all living beings.
verses.wordMeanings: na; hi; vām; viṣamā; dṛṣṭiḥ; suhṛdoḥ; jagat; ātmanoḥ; samayoḥ; sarva; bhūteṣu; bhajantam; bhajatoḥ; api

verses.text: तावाज्ञापयतं भृत्यं किमहं करवाणि वाम् ।पुंसोऽत्यनुग्रहो ह्येष भवद्भ‍िर्यन्नियुज्यते ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāv ajñāpayataṁ bhṛtyaṁkim ahaṁ karavāṇi vāmpuṁso ’ty-anugraho hy eṣabhavadbhir yan niyujyate
verses.meaning: Please order me, Your servant, to do whatever You wish. To be engaged by You in some service is certainly a great blessing for anyone.
verses.wordMeanings: tau; ājñāpayatam; bhṛtyam; kim; aham; karavāṇi; vām; puṁsaḥ; ati; anugrahaḥ; hi; eṣaḥ; bhavadbhiḥ; yat; niyujyate

verses.text: इत्यभिप्रेत्य राजेन्द्र सुदामा प्रीतमानस: ।शस्तै: सुगन्धै: कुसुमैर्माला विरचिता ददौ ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity abhipretya rājendrasudāmā prīta-mānasaḥśastaiḥ su-gandhaiḥ kusumairmālā viracitā dadau
verses.meaning: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] O best of kings, having spoken these words, Sudāmā could understand what Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma wanted. Thus with great pleasure he presented Them with garlands of fresh, fragrant flowers.
verses.wordMeanings: iti; abhipretya; rāja; indra; sudāmā; prīta; mānasaḥ; śastaiḥ; su; gandhaiḥ; kusumaiḥ; malaḥ; viracitāḥ; dadau

verses.text: ताभि: स्वलङ्कृतौ प्रीतौ कृष्णरामौ सहानुगौ ।प्रणताय प्रपन्नाय ददतुर्वरदौ वरान् ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: tābhiḥ sv-alaṅkṛtau prītaukṛṣṇa-rāmau sahānugaupraṇatāya prapannāyadadatur vara-dau varān
verses.meaning: Beautifully adorned with these garlands, Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma were delighted, and so were Their companions. The two Lords then offered the surrendered Sudāmā, who was bowing down before Them, whatever benedictions he desired.
verses.wordMeanings: tābhiḥ; su; alaṅkṛtau; prītau; kṛṣṇa; rāmau; saha; anugau; praṇatāya; prapannāya; dadatuḥ; varadau; varān

verses.text: सोऽपि वव्रेऽचलां भक्तिं तस्मिन्नेवाखिलात्मनि ।तद्भ‍क्तेषु च सौहार्दं भूतेषु च दयां पराम् ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’pi vavre ’calāṁ bhaktiṁtasminn evākhilātmanitad-bhakteṣu ca sauhārdaṁbhūteṣu ca dayāṁ parām
verses.meaning: Sudāmā chose unshakable devotion for Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Soul of all existence; friendship with His devotees; and transcendental compassion for all living beings.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; api; vavre; acalām; bhaktim; tasmin; eva; akhila; ātmani; tat; bhakteṣu; ca; sauhārdam; bhūteṣu; ca; dayām; parām

verses.text: इति तस्मै वरं दत्त्वा श्रियं चान्वयवर्धिनीम् ।बलमायुर्यश: कान्तिं निर्जगाम सहाग्रज: ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti tasmai varaṁ dattvāśriyaṁ cānvaya-vardhinīmbalam āyur yaśaḥ kāntiṁnirjagāma sahāgrajaḥ
verses.meaning: Not only did Lord Kṛṣṇa grant Sudāmā these benedictions, but He also awarded him strength, long life, fame, beauty and ever-increasing prosperity for his family. Then Kṛṣṇa and His elder brother took Their leave.
verses.wordMeanings: iti; tasmai; varam; dattvā; śriyam; ca; anvaya; vardhinīm; balam; āyuḥ; yaśaḥ; kāntim; nirjagāma; saha; agra; jaḥ
verses.purport: We can see a clear difference between Lord Kṛṣṇa’s dealings with the nasty washerman and His dealings with the devoted florist Sudāmā. The Lord is as hard as a thunderbolt for those who defy Him and as soft as a rose for those who surrender to Him. Therefore we should all sincerely surrender to Lord Kṛṣṇa, since that is clearly in our self-interest.
